source.title: A hyperexcited basolateral amygdala complex state determines the hippocampal structural plasticity associated with the reconsolidation of a fear memory

confidence_rationale: Provides causal evidence that BLA state directly determines hippocampal structural plasticity (dendritic spines) during memory reconsolidation. BLA hyperexcitation (via bicuculline) blocks dorsal hippocampus reconsolidation-related remodelling, and NMDA modulation (D-cycloserine) restores it. This establishes the BLA→HPC signalling axis that MECH-074d proposes: BLA emits a remap_signal that triggers hippocampal memory update on harm-PE spike. Confidence moderated by the study using pharmacological manipulation rather than precisely characterising the BLA→HPC signal itself.
failure_signatures: If BLA influence on HPC plasticity operates via downstream amygdala outputs (CeA → brainstem → NE modulation of HPC) rather than direct BLA → HPC projection, the remap_signal architecture in MECH-074d is at the wrong circuit level.
If structural spine changes reflect consolidation per se rather than targeted remapping of specific representations, the attribution-gated partial remap is not supported.

mapping.source_claim_statement: BLA hyperexcitation alters dorsal hippocampus structural plasticity during memory reconsolidation in a manner analogous to stress; NMDA receptor modulation prior to memory reactivation reverses these effects. BLA state critically gates hippocampal memory updating.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-074d proposes that BLAAnalog emits a remap_signal to HippocampalModule when harm-PE exceeds threshold AND attribution gating flags specific latent codes. Sosa et al. provide the biological mechanistic support: BLA state (firing rate, excitability) directly gates whether hippocampal memory traces undergo structural update during reactivation -- the biological equivalent of the remap_signal gating hippocampal code perturbation.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The study does not characterise which hippocampal representations are selectively updated; it measures global dendritic spine density changes, not attribution-specific partial remapping. The 30-40% partial remap claim (Moita 2004) is not addressed.
mapping.source_context: Rat contextual fear conditioning + reconsolidation paradigm; intracranial bicuculline and D-cycloserine infusions; dendritic spine quantification in dorsal hippocampus.

confidence_components.notes: Neuroscience 2025, full paper. Pharmacological causal design is strong; spine quantification is appropriate. Transfer risk: rats, pharmacological manipulation, structural (not functional) plasticity measure.
